title: Questions about Rei in Episode 1
wikitext:
[]: Questions about Rei in Episode 1             Quote Post by Apollonysus » Tue Jul 09, 2013 12:28 am In the Cut#22,the most mysterious scene of Episode 1,Rei's temporary appearance puzzles me a lot. After check the evawiki and the FGC,several questions emerges in my mind. The first question is about the connection between this scene and the scene in EOE I feel little confused with this two scenes.It seems that the cut in EOE echoes the cut in episode1.I still cannot understand why Hideaki arranged a hidden line like that,and what the real message he wanted to give the audiences.Also,the most important,as these two scenes are situated at the beginning and the ending,there must be an inner connection with the whole work which we haven't found.May it hint the main theme like the view of the world in eva or somethings else? Another question is natural.In this cut, you can see the scene is drawn like under a veil,and all the things seems a little vague.Furthermore,there is the detail that Rei doesn't have shadow.So my question is oes Rei really exsist in this scene,or just illusion in Shinji's mind.Does the following cut#23 that the birds suddenly flew away hint that the previous Rei is real? Last but not least,in the wiki page about Rei Ayanami,it writes The very first time we see Rei is Rei 3's appearance to Shinji on the street near the beginning of the episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Jul 09, 2013 12:45 am Welcome to the forum. I like your username. Most people think that it is Rei III, because they think that the goddess (Adam and Lilith together) that Rei III became was beyond time, and existed at all places and times at once. I don't see any reason to think that this is the case, though, and I find it ridiculous to assume that's the intention, especially because there is absolutely no other indication of time travel in the series. As far as I'm concerned, the Rei in episode 1 is just Lilith's eldritch presence manifesting, using the shape her broken soul was most familiar with, and the Rei in episode 26' is just Rei (who is a goddess, mind you) checking in on Shinji and Asuka. Both times it isn't Rei being there physically, just her soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Apollonysus » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:28 am Lavinius wrote: Welcome to the forum. I like your username. Most people think that it is Rei III, because they think that the goddess (Adam and Lilith together) that Rei III became was beyond time, and existed at all places and times at once. I don't see any reason to think that this is the case, though, and I find it ridiculous to assume that's the intention, especially because there is absolutely no other indication of time travel in the series. As far as I'm concerned, the Rei in episode 1 is just Lilith's eldritch presence manifesting, using the shape her broken soul was most familiar with, and the Rei in episode 26' is just Rei (who is a goddess, mind you) checking in on Shinji and Asuka. Both times it isn't Rei being there physically, just her soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:40 am This is the relevant wiki page: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Rei%27s_Ghostly_Appearances_and_Quantum_Mechanics and threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/8420/The-Nature-of-the-Ghostly-Reis-Rei-Lilith-GNR-and-all-that/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/262425/The-first-encounter-with-Rei/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/32/Rei-on-the-street-episode-one/

[]:             Quote Post by Apollonysus » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:58 am Ornette wrote: This is the relevant wiki page: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Rei%27s_Ghostly_Appearances_and_Quantum_Mechanics and threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/8420/The-Nature-of-the-Ghostly-Reis-Rei-Lilith-GNR-and-all-that/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/262425/The-first-encounter-with-Rei/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/32/Rei-on-the-street-episode-one/

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 09, 2013 11:47 am Imagine you're looking down at a piece of paper, spread out on a table. You draw a line on it, from one end to the other. Then, you put your finger on one part of the line, and your other finger on another. From your perspective, you're touching two points on the same thing. From the perspective of a two-dimensional creature traveling down that line, you're appearing, disappearing, and then appearing again. The paper is the universe. The line is Shinji. You're Rei. There you go.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:30 pm Apollonysus wrote: Thanks for your welcome.My username is the combination of Apollo and Dionysus:)I'm also a fan of Nietzsche.

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Tue Jul 09, 2013 3:26 pm Lavinius wrote: As far as I'm concerned, the Rei in episode 1 is just Lilith's eldritch presence manifesting, using the shape her broken soul was most familiar with, and the Rei in episode 26' is just Rei (who is a goddess, mind you) checking in on Shinji and Asuka. Both times it isn't Rei being there physically, just her soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Jul 09, 2013 6:15 pm Muphrid wrote: Going with your idea here, why should Lilith be interested in Shinji at this point in time?

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Tue Jul 09, 2013 11:17 pm That sounds like saying that Rei appears within Shinji's sight in Ep. 1 as mere coincidence due to his proximity to the Angel. That's certainly possible, but to me, it downplays significantly the bookend aspects. It would weaken the strength of the imagery considerably, in my eyes, if it were not intentional that Shinji is the one being watched.

[]:             Quote Post by Apollonysus » Wed Jul 10, 2013 7:14 am Lavinius wrote: I expected as much. Looking closely at the scene in episode one, it doesn't appear to me that the birds flying has anything to do with Rei's appearance. It seems they're just deserting the area before Sachiel's arrival a few moments later. To me it seems that Shinji turns his head to look at the sky, briefly sees Rei in the process, sees the birds, does a re-take, and doesn't see her the second time.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 10, 2013 5:42 pm There are two main suggestions for Rei's "bookend" appearances here. Firstly, following 3I Rei/Lilith gained the ability to transcend both space and time. Her appearance in #01 is regarded as Rei travelling back to observe Shinji at the beginning of the series, or essentially showing that she was observing everyone at all times. This interpretation is based on the many Rei-related references to Quantum mechanics in the show and the general strangeness of that subject. Secondly, as already mentioned, the ghostly Rei's represent the silent "Lilith" part of Rei, projecting itself and observing beyond the bounds of where ever Rei or Lilith are right now. In the case of either or none of these theories, what is clear is that the silent Rei's are observing Shinji . While this has obvious melodramatic effect, I tend to think it has in-universe significance as well, pointing perhaps to a "destiny" for Shinji. Such an interpretation would favour some kind of prescience/time-travel ability on Rei/Lilith's part. That or Rei is subconsciously aware of some preordained plan of Yui's. By the way, it's worth noting that during Toji's hallucination in episode #19 Rei is hinted as having psychic powers of some kind. Rebuild makes this even more explicit, with Rei-Q overtly sending and receiving telepathic messages to and from Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 10, 2013 7:27 pm The mother of all life probably has an innate connection to that life.

[]:             Quote Post by Apollonysus » Thu Jul 11, 2013 11:04 am Her appearance in #01 is regarded as Rei travelling back to observe Shinji at the beginning of the series, or essentially showing that she was observing everyone at all times. This interpretation is based on the many Rei-related references to Quantum mechanics in the show and the general strangeness of that subject.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jul 11, 2013 11:26 am Apollonysus wrote: As we all know, Rei is a clone of Yui made by Gendo. There is no need for Gendo to use Quantum Mechanics to give Rei the ability of time-travel and teleportation.

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Thu Jul 11, 2013 11:35 am The quantum Rei theory is a cool idea, but it involves some extrapolation. What do we know? We know about the ghostly Rei appearances in Ep. 01 and EoE. You could argue that the bookending appearances are different than the harbingers that bring people into Instrumentality, but I will proceed under the assumption they are not substantially different. We know that some of these harbinger avatars appear prior to the merging of Adam and Lilith. Hence, it has been posited that the hybrid can "transcend time," making its presence known throughout time. There are alternatives: maybe these avatars are of Lilith alone all along. That would profoundly change the interpretation of what these avatars represent and mean. It also requires more explanation of the last apparition, which appears after Lilith/the Hybrid should be dead. This strengthens the transcendence theory, or it could suggest Lilith is not really dead. It's possible that they don't have to do with Lilith or the hybrid at all, but given the key role they play in 3I, this seems unlikely. What is the purpose of these avatars? We know the ones in EoE largely serve to bring about Instrumentality. This is one of the reasons you might be justified distinguishing the ones in most of EoE from the bookends, which merely observe. In truth, I think "quantum" is a bit of a misnomer. I'm sure it was so termed for position being uncertain and so on (as well as Rei's room), but transcending time is really not a quantum concept.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 11, 2013 12:45 pm Why does Giant Naked Rei falling apart mean she's dead? She clearly transcends physical form. We see her doing it. Rei can be in many places at once. She's the Kwizatz Haderach. I'm not kidding; kwizatz haderach is derived from "Kefitzat Haderech", a kabbalistic term for teleportation. Rei's final form is a fusion of male and female (Kaworu/Adam and Rei/Lilith), just like the Kwisatz Haderach. Ergo, Rei is a Dune reference.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 11, 2013 1:01 pm The thing is there's more evidence of quantum magic besides that one scene; infact, a couple more solid ones are 1) When Shinji was disembodied into Unit 01, Ritsuko made a point of clarifying his soul is too small to be observed and indeterminate, and 2) When Ritsuko was shot by Gendo in EoE, she saw Rei floating over the water, who was distinct from and imperceptible (probably) to the naked Rei standing by Gendo's side. So, like, did Rei project her soul a few feet just so she could see Ritsuko's "gotcha" face? Why? Given her resemblance to the other incorporeal Reis in the exact movie and their habit of observing the dead and dying, we are obviously supposed to infer that it's one of the projections of Giant Naked Rei. Any other interpretation is weighing against Occam's Razor, and since Giant Naked Rei doesn't exist yet in this scene, GNR must logically be able to project her spirit through time AND space.

[]:             Quote Post by Muphrid » Thu Jul 11, 2013 2:09 pm Chuckman wrote: Why does Giant Naked Rei falling apart mean she's dead? She clearly transcends physical form. We see her doing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Jul 11, 2013 2:23 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Given her resemblance to the other incorporeal Reis in the exact movie and their habit of observing the dead and dying, we are obviously supposed to infer that it's one of the projections of Giant Naked Rei. Any other interpretation is weighing against Occam's Razor, and since Giant Naked Rei doesn't exist yet in this scene, GNR must logically be able to project her spirit through time AND space.
